definition: I2S intercepts a format-type header magic or chunk-type FOURCC comparison and splices the exact literal into the input, routing the parser into the correct format reader or handler.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

members.function: bloaty::Bloaty::ScanAndRollupFile(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bloaty::Rollup*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > > >*) const

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S splices the Mach-O magic bytes and section-name literals intercepted from format-dispatch CMPs directly into the input, enabling build_id extraction that loser WASM-magic inputs can never reach.
signature.one_line: I2S supplies Mach-O magic and section bytes to yield a non-empty build_id

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a Mach-O object file whose magic and section structure let bloaty parse a non-empty build_id.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The leading bytes must form a Mach-O magic constant (ce fa ed fe / cf fa ed fe) followed by enough valid Mach-O load-command/section structure (including __ section-name bytes 5f 5f) that file->GetBuildId() returns a non-empty string, so the true side of if (!build_id.empty()) is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe (or cf fa ed fe) and carry embedded 5f 5f (__) section-name bytes and load-command sizing fields; these parse into a Mach-O whose build_id (UUID) is recovered, populating build_id and exercising lines 1751-1757 only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d (WASM \0asm) or similar non-Mach-O magic; bloaty parses them as a format that yields an empty build_id, so GetBuildId() returns empty and line 1750 always takes the false side (T=0 in L).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the object-file format CMPs (magic and structural-field comparisons) and I2SRandReplace splices the exact required literals (Mach-O magic, section-name bytes) into the input at the offsets where the parser compares them; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct the multi-field magic/structure by blind havoc, so they never produce a parseable build_id.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires a non-empty build_id at the if guard.
build_id is produced by parsing the object file via file->GetBuildId().
Only winner-resolving seeds exercise the true-side body lines 1751-1757.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1750  T=54 F=0  T=0 F=120  if (!build_id.empty()) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe and carry embedded __ (5f 5f) section-name bytes.
Another winner seed uses the alternate Mach-O 64-bit magic cf fa ed fe.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with non-Mach-O magic such as 00 61 73 6d (\0asm).
At the leading magic offsets W and L bytes are sharply concentrated and disjoint (ce/fa/ed/fe vs 00/61/73/6d).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; only its vpc counterpart adds it, matching the second decisive pair direction.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a non-empty build_id behind a Mach-O magic/structure check in which naive (no I2S) resolves the branch at the same rate as cmplog within the budget, showing the magic/structure literals are reachable by blind havoc rather than requiring I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: That bloaty's GetBuildId() returns non-empty specifically for Mach-O (vs other formats) is inferred from the seed magic bytes and source guard, not directly observed from a build_id value in the prompt.

members.source_line: if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == FAT_CIGAM) {

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads the logged magic==MH_MAGIC CMP operand and splices the exact 4-byte Mach-O magic into input offset 0x00-0x03, directly satisfying the equality gate naive never blindly hits.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 4-byte Mach-O magic at offset 0 to pass the format gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the 4-byte Mach-O magic cf fa ed fe so magic equals MH_MAGIC/MH_MAGIC_64/FAT_CIGAM.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes of the input must equal the Mach-O magic word cf fa ed fe at offset 0x00-0x03 so that the read magic compares equal to one of MH_MAGIC / MH_MAGIC_64 / FAT_CIGAM at macho.cc:618, taking the true branch into the MachOObjectFile constructor.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winner-resolving seed begins with the exact bytes cf fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03, which is the little-endian Mach-O magic; the per-offset byte sets show these four offsets are fully concentrated to that value across all 10 winner seeds.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm', the WebAssembly magic), so magic never matches any Mach-O constant and execution falls through to the nullptr return at line 623, exercising the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the logged CMP operands from the magic==MH_MAGIC comparison and splices the constant magic value into the input at the matching offset; naive has no CMP introspection and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte magic, so it never crosses the gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch at macho.cc:618 fires the true side only when magic equals one of three Mach-O magic constants.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == FAT_CIGAM) {

evidence_trail.claim: All winner-resolving seeds carry the Mach-O magic cf fa ed fe in their first four bytes.
The winning seed bytes literally start with cf fa ed fe.
The loser-blocking seeds start with the WebAssembly magic \0asm and not a Mach-O magic.
Loser inputs fall through TryOpenMachOFile to the nullptr return because magic does not match.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte exact-equality magic where naive resolves the gate as often as cmplog (no cmplog>naive divergence), showing the magic byte is reachable by plain havoc rather than requiring I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S signal is a lower-bound floor at depth 3 of one seed's chain; it confirms some I2S contribution but does not prove the specific magic-byte splice was the I2S edit rather than a later havoc refinement.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S intercepts the format-magic comparison, logs the Mach-O magic operand, and substitutes it at offset 0x00-0x03, routing the input into the Mach-O reader where the FindContaining true branch becomes reachable.
signature.one_line: I2S writes Mach-O magic at byte 0 to enter the parser that exposes the gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the Mach-O magic ce-fa-ed-fe at offset 0 so the Mach-O parser populates range maps reached by FindContaining.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first four bytes must equal the Mach-O magic number ce fa ed fe (little-endian 0xFEEDFACE). Bloaty's format dispatch matches this magic to enter the Mach-O object reader, which calls AddRange / FindContaining repeatedly; without the magic match the input is routed to a different (e.g. WASM) parser whose path drives FindContaining only on the false side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with ce fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe (and a few ASCII M/Z/L/E/P/F) at those offsets, and the winners reach FindContaining 562 times and its callers far more, with the blocker true side taken (T=101/160).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM magic '.asm'), so the Mach-O reader is never entered; FindContaining is still invoked 1720 times on the L set but the blocker true side is taken 0 times (T=0 F=1720), i.e. the loser parser path never produces the empty-result condition the true branch encodes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the format-magic comparison and log both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the seed's leading bytes and substitutes the expected magic operand (ce fa ed fe) directly at offset 0. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto the exact 4-byte magic by chance via havoc, which is why naive never resolves (0/10) and value_profile resolves only 1/10.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique and both name I2S as the delta.
The cmplog>naive pair is also an I2S delta.
The blocked side is the true branch of FindContaining's empty-result test.
All winner-resolving seeds start with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe at offset 0.
The W byte set at offsets 0x00-0x03 is concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe while the L byte set is the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d.
Every loser-blocking seed starts with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM '.asm' magic).
On the loser set FindContaining's blocker true side is taken 0 times despite 1720 invocations.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so its only route to the exact magic is chance havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive never resolved the branch and value_profile resolved it only once across ten trials.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates FindContaining behind a 4-byte magic compare shows naive resolving the true branch as often and as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), meaning the magic match is not the controlling feature.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the Mach-O magic at offset 0 is the specific gate (rather than some other 4-byte constant elsewhere in the header) is inferred from the seed-prefix divergence, not from a directly cited source-level magic comparison.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S surfaces the ELF magic to keep the memcmp guard passing, while also exposing that the byte at EI_DATA must avoid both case-label values; cmplog reaches the default arm by landing a non-{1,2} byte that naive keeps setting to a matching case value.
signature.one_line: I2S preserves ELF magic while placing a non-case byte at EI_DATA to hit default

analysis.summary_one_line: Valid ELF magic at bytes 0-3 plus byte 0x05 (EI_DATA) holding a value other than 1 (LSB) or 2 (MSB) to reach the default arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes 0x00-0x03 must equal the literal magic 7f 45 4c 46 (\177ELF) so the memcmp guard at line 452 passes, AND byte 0x05 (ident[EI_DATA]) must hold a value other than ELFDATA2LSB(1) or ELFDATA2MSB(2) so the switch at line 468 falls through to the default arm at line 475.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seeds keep the magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03 and place a non-{1,2} value at offset 0x05 (e.g. seed 1 has 0x30, seed 2 has 0x00, seed 3 has 0xe7), so EI_DATA misses both case labels and hits default.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds also keep the magic but set offset 0x05 to either 0x01 or 0x02 in every sample, so EI_DATA always matches case ELFDATA2LSB or ELFDATA2MSB and the switch never reaches default.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLog buffer for the switch comparison on ident[EI_DATA] and substitutes the recorded compared operand at the input offset, but unlike a single magic-number gate the winning move is to make the byte NOT equal the case labels; I2S surfaces the magic prefix (7f 45 4c 46) keeping line 452 satisfied while the switch on a single byte is escaped via a non-matching value, a combination cmplog reaches and naive's blind havoc rarely lands while preserving the magic.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of the switch on ident[EI_DATA] at elf.cc:475.
Reaching the default requires passing the magic memcmp guard and missing both EI_DATA case labels.
The switch dispatches on ident[EI_DATA] with cases ELFDATA2LSB and ELFDATA2MSB before default.
Winning cmplog seeds preserve the ELF magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03.
A winning seed places a non-{1,2} value (0xe7) at offset 0x05 so EI_DATA misses both case labels.
Loser naive seeds set offset 0x05 to 0x01 or 0x02 (a valid EI_DATA case), so the switch matches a case and never reaches default.
The byte-diff shows W offset 0x05 concentrated on non-{1,2} values while L offset 0x05 is dominated by 01/02.
The divergent-branches table shows the blocker default at line 475 taken only in W (T=60) and never in L (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 475  T=60 F=0  T=0 F=2220  default:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other CMP operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch dispatches on a single byte gated behind a preceding magic memcmp showing naive resolving the default arm as often as cmplog (no cmplog>naive divergence), which would mean I2S substitution of the magic prefix is not what enables the winner.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether I2S's advantage here is solving the magic memcmp prefix (a roadblock) versus the switch byte being escaped by chance, since reaching default requires a NON-matching byte that I2S does not directly synthesize.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S intercepts the switch CMP on e_machine, logs the EM_X86_64 constant 0x3e, and substitutes it at input offset 0x12-0x13; naive must blindly guess the 2-byte value and almost never does.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes EM_X86_64 constant into e_machine field to hit the switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF header e_machine field (2 bytes at offset 0x12) must equal EM_X86_64 (0x003e) to take the case EM_X86_64 switch arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 2-byte little-endian e_machine field at input offset 0x12-0x13 must hold 0x003e (62 = EM_X86_64). The switch on e_machine at elf.cc:782 dispatches the true arm only for this exact 16-bit value.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seed 2a25258f656af261 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x12-0x13 (little-endian 0x003e = EM_X86_64), and seed 80ff9a7dac09a5e8 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x13-0x14; both supply the exact comparand the switch tests for.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds carry 00 at offset 0x12, never the 0x3e byte that the switch arm requires, so they fall through to the default arm and return false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the switch comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp callbacks, logs the constant 0x3e operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact byte into the input at the e_machine offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must hit 0x003e by blind havoc mutation, which it almost never does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the case EM_X86_64 arm of a switch on e_machine.
The true side of line 787 (case EM_X86_64) is the globally blocked side.
Winning cmplog seed carries 3e 00 at the e_machine offset 0x12-0x13.
W bytes at offset 0x12 include 0x3e while L bytes there are dominated by 00.
Loser naive seed holds 00 at offset 0x12 and never the EM_X86_64 byte.
The loser takes the default arm and returns false at line 822/828.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 822  T=0 F=6  T=26 F=3  default:

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile records a Hamming-distance CMP_MAP bucket and preserves inputs closer to matching as corpus.

An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolved the branch in all 10 trials versus naive's 1.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 16-bit equality constant where cmplog does NOT resolve faster than naive, or where naive resolves the EM_X86_64 switch arm at the same 10/10 rate without any CMP-derived stepping-stone.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile gradient story rests on mechanism description only; no per-step CMP_MAP-bucket trace is shown for the value_profile winner, so the gradient-vs-lucky-havoc distinction for Pair 2 is inferred rather than directly observed.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S logs the EM_AARCH64 constant 0xB7 from the switch CMP callbacks and substitutes it at input offset 0x12, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step that naive never hits by blind mutation.
signature.one_line: I2S writes EM_AARCH64 byte into e_machine offset to dispatch the correct switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF e_machine field must equal EM_AARCH64 (0x00B7 little-endian) at the 16-bit machine offset to take the switch arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ELF header e_machine half-word must hold the exact 16-bit value EM_AARCH64 = 0x00B7 (low byte 0xB7, high byte 0x00) so that `switch (e_machine)` dispatches to `case EM_AARCH64:` at elf.cc:798. In the winner this lands at input bytes 0x12=0xB7, 0x13=0x00.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed carries the bytes 01 d5 b7 00 at offsets 0x10-0x13, placing 0xB7 at offset 0x12; the per-role overlay marks line 798 case EM_AARCH64 as [W] (winner-only) and the divergent-branch table shows W takes the true side (T=3 F=0) while L never does (T=0 F=29).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed misses the EM_AARCH64 constant: the byte-diff shows offset 0x12 is 0xB7 only in W while L holds 00x8/03/01, so the switch falls through to `default:` (line 822, marked [L]) which returns false; the divergent-branch table shows L takes default true 26 times and EM_AARCH64 never.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CMP-interception logs the `e_machine == EM_AARCH64` integer comparison operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the observed operand and substitutes the magic 0xB7 directly at the matching offset, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step. naive has only havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it must blind-guess the 2-byte magic and never lands it across 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case EM_AARCH64 switch arm whose true side is globally blocked.
The arm is reached only by an exact-equality switch on e_machine.
The winner seed places 0xB7 at offset 0x12 within the ELF header.
Only the winner exercises the EM_AARCH64 arm; line 798 is winner-only in the per-role overlay.
Winner takes the EM_AARCH64 true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 798  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=29  case EM_AARCH64:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: At offset 0x12 the magic byte 0xB7 is present only in W; loser seeds hold other values.
Loser seeds fall through to the default arm rather than EM_AARCH64.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 822  T=0 F=3  T=26 F=3  default:

`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability — only havoc/token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the EM_AARCH64 16-bit magic where naive resolves the branch as often as cmplog, or where cmplog fails to resolve it — showing the I2S CMP substitution is not what solves the exact-equality gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The decisive seed was stored by cmplog with a BytesCopyMutator leaf op, so the I2S substitution is inferred from dash-row ancestors rather than a labeled I2SRandReplace mutation directly on the magic byte.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the chunk-type CMP operand and substitutes the 4-byte tag (iTXt) that routes execution into a zlib-inflate path returning Z_OK, landing on the case Z_OK switch arm that naive never reaches.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes chunk-type tag to route through Z_OK switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: A 4-byte chunk-type tag at offset 0x35 must select a zlib-decompressing chunk whose inflate returns Z_OK in png_zstream_error.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A specific 4-byte PNG chunk-type FOURCC at file offset 0x35-0x38 (e.g. iTXt = 69 54 58 74 or kRGB-like variant) that routes the input into a zlib-inflate code path whose return code is Z_OK, so png_zstream_error(Z_OK) is reached and the true side of `case Z_OK:` (L1007) is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry distinct chunk-type bytes at 0x35-0x38 (iTXt / kRGB) differing from the loser, and the per-role overlay marks line 1007 case Z_OK as [W] winner-only while the loser sits on [L] Z_STREAM_END / Z_BUF_ERROR arms — the winner exercises the exact ret==Z_OK switch arm 12 times (T=12 F=0) where the loser never does (T=0 F=225).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds hold the chunk type sRGB (73 52 47 42) at the same offset 0x35-0x38, which routes to chunk paths whose zlib calls return Z_STREAM_END or Z_BUF_ERROR instead of Z_OK, so they land on case Z_STREAM_END (L1011, T=216) and case Z_BUF_ERROR (L1041, T=9) and never on case Z_OK.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the chunk-type CMP operands were logged, finds the loser's chunk-type bytes in the input, and substitutes the operand that drives execution toward the Z_OK-returning chunk path; naive lacks I2S and must blunder onto the right 4-byte tag by havoc alone, which it fails to do in 8/10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is case Z_OK at png.c:1007, true side globally blocked, inside png_zstream_error.
cmplog resolves the branch 9/10 while naive resolves 0/10, with I2S as the technique delta.
The winner-only overlay puts case Z_OK on the W (winner-resolving) path.
Winner takes the Z_OK arm (T=12 F=0) while loser never does (T=0 F=225) and instead takes Z_STREAM_END and Z_BUF_ERROR.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1007  T=12 F=0  T=0 F=225  case Z_OK:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser routes to the Z_STREAM_END switch arm 216 times, where the winner never goes.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1011  T=0 F=12  T=216 F=9  case Z_STREAM_END:

evidence_trail.claim: At offset 0x35-0x38 the winner carries iTXt/kRGB chunk-type bytes while the loser carries sRGB.
The 4-byte tag is a chunk type; winner seed bytes show iTXt at offset 0x35.
Loser naive seeds carry the sRGB chunk type at the same offset.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S, only havoc and token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a 4-byte chunk-type literal at a fixed offset where naive resolves it as fast as cmplog (no cmplog>naive separation), showing the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of that 4-byte tag.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the 0x35-0x38 chunk type to a zlib path returning specifically Z_OK is inferred from switch-arm hit counts and source, not directly traced through the inflate return value.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 4-byte chunk_name==png_PLTE CMP via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp4, logs the PLTE FOURCC constant, and splices it into the chunk-type bytes; naive's havoc cannot brute-force the exact 4-byte tag.
signature.one_line: I2S splices 4-byte PLTE FOURCC into chunk-type field via CMP interception

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the PLTE FOURCC so chunk_name == png_PLTE holds true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte chunk-type field decodes to png_PLTE (ASCII 'PLTE' = 0x50 0x4c 0x54 0x45), making the equality test chunk_name == png_PLTE at pngread.c:159 evaluate true and dispatch png_handle_PLTE.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and hits the true side 293 times on average; its I2SRandReplace mutator reads the failing 4-byte chunk_name comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the png_PLTE constant directly into the chunk-type bytes, so a PLTE chunk appears in the input.

hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks in 9/10 trials with avg hitcount 0 on the blocker; lacking I2SRandReplace it relies on CMP_MAP prefix-distance feedback and havoc, which cannot reliably reconstruct the exact 4-byte 'IHDR' literal in a second chunk-type slot, so png_handle_IHDR is only entered once and (mode & PNG_HAVE_IHDR) is still zero.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (vpc-only mutator) substitutes the logged chunk-type comparand 'IHDR' directly into a candidate chunk-type field, deterministically producing the duplicate-IHDR structure that drives the blocker's true (out-of-place) branch; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact literal.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of the duplicate/out-of-place IHDR guard.
Taking the true branch leads to the out-of-place chunk error, confirming the branch fires only on a second IHDR.
PNG_HAVE_IHDR is set inside png_handle_IHDR, so it is non-zero only on a re-entry from a second IHDR chunk.
value_profile_cmplog wins decisively (10/10) over value_profile (1/10), the only technique delta being I2S.
The winner reaches the blocker repeatedly while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=20  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry the IHDR chunk-type literal in their bytes.
value_profile_cmplog has the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks.
value_profile explicitly has no I2SRandReplace.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only roadblock is a duplicate-IHDR-style exact 4-byte magic-literal chunk type would be refuted if value_profile resolved the branch as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing CMP_MAP prefix-distance feedback alone can reconstruct the literal without I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact dataflow path by which a duplicate 'IHDR' chunk-type is produced is inferred from source structure and the IHDR literal in seeds; the byte-diff offsets do not pinpoint a second IHDR tag, so the duplicate-chunk construction is the most uncertain claim.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 'tRNS' FOURCC into the chunk-type field, causing png_handle_tRNS to execute and set num_trans non-zero; the compound condition at L4533-L4534 then evaluates true, which naive's blind havoc never achieves.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'tRNS' FOURCC to set num_trans non-zero for compound check

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a parseable tRNS chunk so png_ptr->num_trans is set non-zero, with PNG_EXPAND active.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG whose chunk stream includes a valid tRNS chunk (FOURCC 0x74 0x52 0x4e 0x53 = 'tRNS') that png_handle_tRNS parses, setting png_ptr->num_trans != 0, combined with an expand transformation so (png_ptr->transformations & PNG_EXPAND) != 0 — making the compound condition at L4533/L4534 true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds drive png_handle_tRNS 10 times (W hits) vs 0 in the loser, and the blocker L4533 evaluates true in all 10 winner trials (T=10 F=0); the upstream num_trans checks L4459 and L4477 also take their true sides only in the winner. The winner byte sets at the chunk-FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 carry non-default values (e.g. 69/54/58/74) showing chunk-type bytes were mutated toward real ancillary chunks.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds never invoke png_handle_tRNS (L hits = 0), so num_trans stays zero, and the blocker L4533 evaluates false in all 10 loser trials (T=0 F=10); at the FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 every loser seed keeps the default 67 41 4d 41 ('gAMA') bytes with no tRNS variant.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the FOURCC comparison in png_read_chunk dispatch, reads the expected 'tRNS' literal from the CmpLog buffer, and substitutes it into input bytes at the chunk-type offset, manufacturing a parseable tRNS chunk that sets num_trans non-zero; naive must hit the exact 4-byte FOURCC by blind havoc and never does in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the num_trans!=0 condition at L4533, requiring num_trans non-zero (with PNG_EXPAND).
The full compound gate also requires PNG_EXPAND set on the next source line.
The winner takes the blocker true side in all 10 trials while the loser takes false in all 10.
png_handle_tRNS fires 10 times in the winner set and 0 times in the loser set, so num_trans is set only in the winner.
Upstream num_trans check L4477 takes its true side only in the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4477  T=8 F=0  T=0 F=0  if (png_ptr->num_trans != 0)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry mutated chunk-FOURCC bytes at offsets 0x25-0x28 while losers keep the default gAMA bytes.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies on blind havoc/token byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a naive-only campaign reaches the L4533 true side at the same rate as cmplog within the budget (showing blind havoc can place the 4-byte tRNS FOURCC unaided), the I2S attribution is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff evidence ties winner seeds to mutated chunk-FOURCC bytes but does not directly prove the substituted literal is exactly 'tRNS' rather than another expand-triggering ancillary chunk.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace splices the logged 'pHYs' chunk-type comparand directly into the input chunk-type bytes, manufacturing the FOURCC that routes into png_handle_pHYs; value_profile must blunder all four exact bytes by chance and consistently fails to do so.
signature.one_line: I2S writes pHYs FOURCC into chunk-type field to enable handler dispatch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a pHYs chunk (FOURCC 'pHYs' = 70 48 59 73) so png_handle_pHYs is dispatched and the length!=9 test is reached.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the ASCII literal 'pHYs' (70 48 59 73). The PNG chunk-type dispatcher only calls png_handle_pHYs when the chunk type matches 'pHYs'; without that exact FOURCC the code at pngrutil.c:2180 (if (length != 9)) is never reached, let alone resolved.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed 2 (vpc, trial 1) carries the literal bytes 70 48 59 73 ('pHYs') at offset 0x25-0x28, so the dispatcher invokes png_handle_pHYs and the branch is exercised 103 times; W reaches line 2180 with T=15/F=6.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 67 41 4d 41 ('gAMA') at offset 0x25-0x28 and no pHYs FOURCC, so png_handle_pHYs is never dispatched; L records T=0 F=13 at line 2180 (the branch is never reached) and the loser blocks 9/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: the chunk-type comparison against the 'pHYs' constant is logged in the CmpLogObserver, and I2SRandReplace splices that logged 4-byte constant directly into the input where the (mutated) chunk-type bytes sit, manufacturing a valid 'pHYs' FOURCC in seconds. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and must blunder the exact 4-byte token by chance, which it fails to do.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the length test inside png_handle_pHYs and the globally-blocked side is the true branch.
A winner-resolving seed carries the literal 'pHYs' FOURCC at offset 0x25.
Loser-blocking seeds carry 'gAMA' rather than 'pHYs' at the same chunk-type offset.
The winner reaches and exercises line 2180 while the loser never reaches it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2180  T=15 F=6  T=0 F=13  if (length != 9)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The branch dispatch into png_handle_pHYs is gated on the chunk type and the byte-diff shows winners flipping the chunk-type bytes toward a different FOURCC.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace while value_profile does not, which is the single technique delta of the decisive pair.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile with an I2S technique delta and large divergence.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the pHYs chunk-type comparison is replaced by a runtime-derived (non-input-byte) dispatch key showing value_profile_cmplog losing its advantage over value_profile would refute the I2S-substitution mechanism; conversely, if value_profile resolves the branch at the same rate as vpc once an exact-FOURCC token gate is present, the I2S attribution is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only one of five winner seeds shows the literal 'pHYs' FOURCC (the rest carry 'gAMA'), so the byte evidence that the FOURCC is the resolving feature rather than a co-occurring length/IHDR change rests on a single seed plus the dispatch-gating source logic.
